title: USS Eisenhower Carrier Strike Group intercepts Houthi drone and missile attack targeting commercial shipping in Red Sea
summary: US 5th Fleet reported that USS Dwight D. Eisenhower Carrier Strike Group intercepted and destroyed two anti-ship ballistic missiles and four one-way attack drones launched from Houthi-controlled Yemen toward commercial vessels in the southern Red Sea.
contents:
# USS Eisenhower CSG Intercepts Houthi Attack in Red Sea

## Incident Report
US 5th Fleet confirmed that the USS Dwight D. Eisenhower Carrier Strike Group intercepted multiple threats targeting commercial shipping in the southern Red Sea.

## Threats Engaged
- 2x anti-ship ballistic missiles launched from Houthi-controlled territory
- 4x one-way attack drones (Iranian-made Shahed variants)
- All threats destroyed before reaching commercial vessels
- No damage to US Navy ships or commercial shipping
- No casualties

## Engagement Details
- **USS Gravely** (DDG-107) engaged and destroyed the ballistic missiles using SM-6 interceptors
- **USS Mason** (DDG-87) intercepted three drones using SM-2 missiles and CIWS
- F/A-18 Super Hornets from CVW-3 engaged and destroyed one drone in flight
- Threat origin: Multiple launch sites in Houthi-controlled western Yemen

## 5th Fleet Statement
Commander, US Naval Forces Central Command stated: "These attacks on international shipping remain unacceptable. Our forces stand ready to defend freedom of navigation and protect commercial vessels transiting this critical waterway."

## Context
This engagement marks the 15th Houthi attack on Red Sea shipping in May 2026, continuing a pattern of escalating threats to the Bab el-Mandeb chokepoint that has disrupted global supply chains since late 2023.
